description_long: This object is a pointed, leaf-shaped stone with a dark gray coloration and a rough, chipped surface, resembling a hand-crafted tool or projectile point. The stone tapers to a sharp tip at one end and widens toward the base, with visible flaking marks along its edges, suggesting it was shaped by deliberate knapping. One side features a slightly convex surface, while the opposite side is flatter, and there is a small area with white painted or inscribed markings. The edges are irregular and jagged, indicating it is not a naturally smooth stone but rather one that has been intentionally worked. The overall form is symmetrical along its length, with a central ridge running from base to tip. Its size and shape suggest it could be used as an obstacle or decorative stone in an urban park or garden.
description: A dark gray, leaf-shaped stone with a rough, chipped surface and a pointed tip, featuring visible flaking marks and a small white inscription.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed, symmetrical shape with a rough, flaked surface and a sharp tip.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin, blade-like profile and the uneven, chipped edges.
description_view_2: Back view displays a flatter surface with a white marking and similar flaking patterns as the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view highlights the narrow thickness and jagged edge contours.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or embedded in gravel or soil
attribute_pattern: Irregular flaking and chipping patterns from knapping, with a small area of white painted or inscribed marking; no regular or natural veining.
attribute_weathering: Rough and chipped, with visible tool marks; minimal natural weathering, suggesting recent shaping or limited outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces; may be partially embedded in soil or gravel, standing out as a distinct feature in landscaped settings.
